checks.name: schema_completeness
checks.detail: All required payload fields present. Includes customSections (communication_protocols, priority_classification, linear_labeling) — bonus structured content.

checks.name: flow_integrity
checks.detail: 3 flows: flow_1 (New Conversation Processing, 15 nodes/17 edges), flow_2 (Weekly Feedback Digest, 8 nodes/7 edges), flow_3 (Ambiguous Manual Review, 10 nodes/10 edges). All well-connected with error handling.

checks.name: trigger_validity
checks.detail: 2 triggers: polling (120s interval) and schedule (0 17 * * 5 weekly Friday 5 PM). Both valid.

checks.name: adoption_question_fitness
checks.detail: 5 adoption_questions added: slack_channel, linear_default_team, dedup_confidence, review_timeout_hours, polling_interval

checks.name: safety_scan
checks.detail: 0 critical findings. Ambiguous items flagged for human review. Never silently merges duplicates. Paper trail via comments.

dimensions.name: Prompt Completeness
dimensions.rationale: Identity 55+ words. Instructions cover 6 detailed sections: polling, classification (5 categories), deduplication with memory, Linear issue creation with labeling taxonomy, Slack summaries, and weekly digest. Tool guidance covers Intercom, Linear (GraphQL), Slack APIs. 3 examples (new feature, duplicate bug, ambiguous). Error handling covers API failures, data issues, state corruption. Custom sections add priority guide and labeling taxonomy.

dimensions.name: Tool-Prompt Alignment
dimensions.rationale: All tools mapped: http_request for Intercom REST, Linear GraphQL, Slack REST. file_read/file_write for state (last_poll.json, known_issues.json, processing_log.json, failed_creates.json). Linear GraphQL mutations and queries documented. No phantom tools.

dimensions.name: Trigger Coherence
dimensions.rationale: 120-second polling matches the real-time conversation processing requirement. Friday 5 PM weekly digest provides periodic summary. Both triggers align with prompt descriptions.

dimensions.name: Connector Coverage
dimensions.rationale: All 3 service_flow entries fully backed by connectors. Intercom access_token, Linear API key, Slack bot_token — all with setup instructions.

dimensions.name: Memory Design
dimensions.rationale: Excellent: known_issues.json maps keywords to Linear issue IDs with report_count and timestamps. Processing log for audit trail. 90-day pruning during weekly digest. Multi-layer deduplication (local memory + Linear API search).

dimensions.name: Error Handling
dimensions.rationale: Covers Intercom 429/401/403, Linear create failures with retry queue, Slack message queuing, state file corruption recovery, empty bodies, missing customer info, malformed JSON. Low-confidence deduplication handled with 'possibly related' notes.

dimensions.name: Use Case Fidelity
dimensions.rationale: All 3 flows fully covered: polling cycle with classification and deduplication, weekly digest with trend analysis, and ambiguous feedback manual review with escalation timeout.

dimensions.name: Value Clarity
dimensions.rationale: Clear outcome: intelligent feedback triage replacing 4 separate workflows. Deduplication prevents issue spam. Priority classification saves product team time. Weekly digest surfaces trends.

dimensions.name: Execution Feasibility
dimensions.rationale: Intercom, Linear, Slack APIs are standard. Linear GraphQL adds slight complexity but is well-documented. Polling with checkpoint is practical. 120-second interval is reasonable.

dimensions.name: Differentiation
dimensions.rationale: Semantic deduplication, 5-category classification with confidence scoring, priority assignment based on impact and frequency, ambiguous item escalation — this is exactly what an AI agent does better than rigid automation.

dimensions.name: Variable Necessity
dimensions.rationale: All 5 questions target meaningful config: team routing, dedup sensitivity, review timeout, polling frequency. Each directly affects agent behavior.

dimensions.name: Default Quality
dimensions.rationale: Good defaults for channel (#product-feedback), dedup_confidence (70%), review_timeout (48h), polling_interval (120s). Only linear_default_team needs user input. Very close to zero-config adoption.

issues: No adoption_questions existed — 5 added covering Slack, Linear team, dedup tuning, review timeout, and polling config
Intercom and Linear connectors may not have builtin definitions

template: Added 5 adoption_questions: slack_channel, linear_default_team, dedup_confidence, review_timeout_hours, polling_interval
Included use_case_ids on review_timeout_hours (flow_3) and polling_interval (flow_1) to scope them to relevant flows
Used select type with options for dedup_confidence, review_timeout_hours, and polling_interval for constrained choices
